title: How to Automatically Move Leads to Contacts and Link Them to a Company?

posts.author: MLisek (MLisek)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

I have three boards in Monday.com: Leads, Contacts, and Companies.
The Leads board contains the following data: First Name, Last Name, Email, Company Name.

I would like to set up an automation that performs the following steps:

  1. Moves an existing entry from the Leads board to the Contacts board.
  2. Creates a new entry in the Companies board (if the company does not already exist) based on the data stored in the Leads board.
  3. Automatically links the newly created company to the transferred contact.

Does anyone know the best way to set up this automation? Would it be possible using built-in Monday automations or integrations?

Thanks in advance for any guidance!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: What trigger to create a contact? ie status change or button click?

posts.author: MLisek (MLisek)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi
A new contact is created by pressing the button.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Do you have the workflow center or just automations? Workflow center would be easiest, but can be done with a few automations. ie When button pressed, move the lead to the contact. Then on the Contacts board use the template

image337×452 13.2 KB

posts.author: MLisek (MLisek)
posts.cookedHtml:
Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador:

Do you have the workflow center or just automations?

Can you write something more? Which option is this? Is that what you mean? https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/11065311570066-Get-started-with-monday-workflows

posts.author: MLisek (MLisek)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for your answer, I will check. I took the liberty of making a video showing the goal I want to achieve. I did everything manually, now I am looking for a way to automate it. Link to the video https://youtu.be/L1jKMR3eLBs

posts.author: MLisek (MLisek)
posts.cookedHtml: I forgot to add that the idea is to use solutions provided by monday.com. I want to avoid paid add-ons.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for that. The video shows you don’t have workflows (it’s still in beta). So you’ll need to use automations ie add a column that includes an automation that when clicked, it moves the item to the next board. Then use automation on the next board to connect to to existing company. Have you used automations previously?

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @MLisek

We can set up a seamless workflow to move leads to contacts, create new companies if they don’t exist, and automatically link them together.

When a lead is moved to a specific group or status in the Leads board.
Use an automation recipe or an integration tool like Make.com or Zapier to move the lead to the Contacts board.
Check if the company already exists in the Companies board. If not, create a new entry.
Link the newly created company to the contact using a connection column.

posts.author: MLisek (MLisek)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you, but before we enter the company’s 3 tool, the assumption is to use native monday solutions.
I’ll save it for the future, the option to check if something already exists is interesting

posts.author: MLisek (MLisek)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, I know automation and I can work with them… The problem I have is that I have no idea which automation to use on which board. And here’s a request to you…
On the Leads board, use the buttons and automation “x”
On the Contacts board, use automation “y”
On the Companies board, use automation “z” to connect to the Contacts board

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
On leads - when button clicked, move item to Contacts Boards
On Contacts - you should be able to use these template automations to do what you need.
